description: Partnership agreement. Location: Cairo. Dated 1667/1668 CE (״חכתה״ = 5428) between Yaʿaqov ha-Levi and a certain Yosef and a certain Refa'el. This dating "חכתה" matches the surviving final two digits of the year (28) in the line just before the witness signatures. The surviving section of the document represents the middle of what could be, at least, three fragments. The exact purpose of the partnership agreement is unclear due to damage, but some of the key elements are apparent. (1) There were three members of the partnership: Yaʿaqov ha-Levi Naḥman, Yosef [surname unknown], and Rafaʾel Menashe ha-Levi. Yosef and Yaʿaqov were likely brothers (l. 6), unless a fourth name appeared in the missing opening lines of the document. The partners had some kind of financial dealings with Shemuʾel Menashe, who is now deceased and they are now settling debts with his son Ḥayyīm instead. All of these details appear as context before the document states its purpose in line 26, possibly a dissolution (but this reading is tentative due to the level of damage). This section of the document also mentions an unnamed widow and orphan, but their role is also unclear. Only the signature of the consenting party Rafaʾel Menashe ha-Levi survives. Below that there is an addendum mentioning the deceased man Shemuʾel Menashe and his son Ḥayyīm. The verso also contains another related addendum as well as brief recycled(?) text halfway down the right border. The ink distribution of this unidentified text points to the possibility that the paper consists of two recycled pieces that were pasted together, one with text that was revealed by the tear. MCD.
description_he: הסכם שותפות; מקום: קהיר; תאריך: 1667/1668 לספירה (תכ'ח); בין יעקב הלוי, יוסף מסוים ורפאל מנשה הלוי; עוסק בחיסול חשבונות עם יורשו של שמואל מנשה המנוח
